items.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

articles.title: Outbreak of highly contagious infection grows to more than 220 cases

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

articles.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

gallery.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

thumbnail.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

items.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets family of Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.

image.altText: Albo has arms around an Indigenous woman wearing scarf, holding pink toy, a woman has head on another's shoulder. All serious.

items.title: Core Lithium restarts Northern Territory mine as price rallies

children.content: Core Lithium has restarted mining at its project near Darwin as prices surge close to $4,200 a tonne.

image.altText: an aerial photo of a mining processing plant.

items.title: NT Police officer acquitted of coercing ex-partner over T-shirt assault

children.content: A Northern Territory Police officer has been cleared of coercing his former partner into withdrawing an assault charge after facing trial on one count of attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A group of people in suits walking out of the NT Supreme Court.

items.title: Man charged with Arnhem Land murder bailed to live with family 500km away

children.content: A 20-year-old man charged with the murder of another man during a brawl in Arnhem Land last month has been bailed to live with family far from the location of the death, in the Tiwi Islands.

image.altText: An aerial view of Milingimbi community in north-east Arnhem Land.

items.title: How a 'partial obscuration' phenomenon is impacting Darwin's sunsets

children.content: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.

gallery.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Large clouds obscure the setting sun, casting shadows of blue sky over the rays of sunlight.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

thumbnail.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Large clouds obscure the setting sun, casting shadows of blue sky over the rays of sunlight.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

items.title: Former NT child protection staff speak out after Kumanjayi Little Baby case

children.content: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

image.altText: Aboriginal woman, brown/gray hair tied back, blue pattern deress, serious expression, sitting, blurred greenery behind her

items.title: NT records two deaths from rare mosquito-borne disease

children.content: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A female Aedes aegypti mosquito bites someone's skin.

items.title: Calls for hastily constructed NT evacuation site to be relocated

children.content: Residents in the regional NT town of Batchelor say they have been left in the dark about the future of a pop-up evacuation centre that was built earlier this year without their consultation for flood evacuees.

gallery.altText: A mattress hangs over a railing with generators in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A mattress hangs over a railing with generators in the background.

items.title: Married at First Sight UK allegations

children.content: A British network has entirely removed Married at First Sight UK from air and its streaming channel after allegations of rape and sexual assault.
The show is widely seen as controversial with accusations it normalises or even platforms toxic relationships.
Here's what we know about the allegations so far.

image.altText: A blonde woman wearing a blue top sits at a desk, speaking directly at a camea.

gallery.altText: A blonde woman wearing a blue top sits at a desk, speaking directly at a camea.

thumbnail.altText: A blonde woman wearing a blue top sits at a desk, speaking directly at a camea.

items.title: Greenland tells US again it is 'not for sale' after Trump envoy talks

children.content: Jeff Landry, appointed by Donald Trump in December as the US special envoy to Greenland, has met the country's prime minister in the capital Nuuk.

image.altText: Lines of snow-covered houses seen on the edge of a snowy coastline, alongside dark ocean water.

items.title: Maldives investigates if Italian divers went too deep in fatal cave dive

children.content: A Maldives government official says a permit was given to study soft corals, but authorities were unaware the group planned to cave dive.

image.altText: divers

items.title: Australian government calls for detained flotilla members to be released

children.content: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

image.altText: a war boat driving towards photographer

items.title: Man who subjected surfer to 'acts of humiliation' jailed over killing

children.content: Guy Haymes died after being assaulted in a dispute over $50 at a Manly apartment in February 2024.

image.altText: A middle-aged man wearing a cap smiles at the camera.

images.altText: Two men shake hands in front of Chinese and Russian flags

children.content: The two leaders are expected to discuss bilateral economic relations and "key international and regional issues", according to the Kremlin. Follow live.

updates.title: Signing ceremony up next as large-scale meeting ends
Rare joint press meeting expected following talks

items.title: The men offering an alternative to 'manosphere' influencers

children.content: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?

image.altText: A selfie of Caleb Ferdinand and his toddler, taken inside a car.

items.title: What life is like as an older parent, or being born to one

children.content: After publishing our story on having babies in your 40s, we heard from readers on what being, or having, an older parent is like.

image.altText: couple with pram on sidewalk

items.title: Teenager Bianca Adler becomes youngest Australian to summit Everest

children.content: A social media post just before 8am AEST announced that Bianca Adler had reached the top of the world's tallest mountain.

image.altText: A girl wearing a helmet with stickets in a purple jacket holds a pick ice pick close to the camera, smiling widely.

items.title: Teenaged trio arrested over Melbourne nightclub arsons

children.content: Victoria Police allege the three males are responsible for arson attacks at nightclubs La Di Da and Electric Bar earlier this month.

image.altText: A man being arrested by two people

items.title: Life on one of the world's most dangerous waterways

children.content: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.

gallery.altText: three women in the shallows of a beach swimming and taking photos.

thumbnail.altText: three women in the shallows of a beach swimming and taking photos.

items.title: One killed, four injured in 'brazen, violent attack' inside Sydney home

children.content: A man with links to rap group OneFour has died and four others have been seriously injured following a Canley Heights shooting, which is believed to be organised crime-related.

image.altText: Police officers in suburban street and inside a brick home

items.title: Infant death sparks call for use of church land to tackle homelessness

children.content: Church land is being touted as a potential solution to the homelessness crisis in a southern NSW city still reeling from the death of a newborn baby in a tent almost three weeks ago.

image.altText: Homeless setup near Wagga beach with backdrop of church

items.title: Big guns return as Matildas call up teen goalkeeper

children.content: Mary Fowler, Ellie Carpenter and Steph Catley are back in the Matildas squad for games against Mexico, while a teenager has been called up.

gallery.altText: Mary Fowler runs with the ball

thumbnail.altText: Mary Fowler runs with the ball

items.title: ASX falls below 8,500 level as rate rise fears surface

children.content: The Australian share market has fallen below 8,500 points after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it. Follow live.

image.altText: Shares on a screen at the ASX

items.title: Mosque shooting victims hailed as heroes and mourned by community

children.content: Three people killed by two shooters at a San Diego mosque were beloved pillars of the community and died while saving 140 children in the building at the time of the attack, authorities say.

image.altText: Images of three smiling men in separate black digital TV frames, alongside US flags.

items.title: Miners plan to retrofit WA nickel plants to cash in on high gold prices

children.content: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

image.altText: A mining executive in high-vis standing in front of a mill.

items.title: Apprehension as UK mining company enters Australian coal

children.content: Anglo American has operated metallurgical coal mines in Queensland since the turn of the century. Now, a UK-based company has bought them all.

image.altText: A man in an orange high-vis uniform and helmet stands in front of a large truck and pile of rocks

items.title: More ISIS-linked women and children set to return to Australia

children.content: Six Australian women, along with their children and grandchildren, look likely to leave a camp in Syria within days.

gallery.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

items.title: Luai and Tigers headline NRL's Las Vegas quartet for 2027 season-openers

children.content: Jarome Luai will begin his final season with the Wests Tigers in Las Vegas, with the NRL unveiling its teams for next year's season-openers.

image.altText: Jarome Luai lookes dejected after a Wests Tigers' loss during the 2025 NRL season.

items.title: Three Aussies in Giro d'Italia top 10 after key time trial

children.content: Ben O'Connor uses his Perth experience to impress in the Giro d'Italia's only time trial stage of 2026, leapfrogging fellow West Australian Jai Hindley in the overall standings, with another Perth boy Michael Storer just behind them.

image.altText: Ben O'Connor rides his bike

items.title: Government businesses defy premier's call for transparency

children.content: Tasmanian government-owned businesses or companies are being threatened with legislation to force them to disclose right-to-information documents publicly after nine of them failed to respond to the premier's request.

image.altText: Man looks out to the distance, leaning on a green chair

items.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

image.altText: A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt

items.title: Worm living in a 'glass castle' among thousands of new marine species

children.content: Thousands of species have been discovered lurking and thriving in our oceans, as part of an effort to catalogue 100,000 new species.

gallery.altText: A bulbous tree stretches from the sand, hanging in the water.
A strange looking worm multi-coloured with prongs stretching out from its body.
